user_title: COO & Global Head of Change Execution, WPS

talking_points.headline: HSBC Wealth $39bn Q1 Inflows — H2 WPS Capacity Must Match the Momentum
talking_points.why_it_matters: HSBC's US$39bn Q1 net new money — 87% from Asia — validates WPS strategy execution, but raises the H2 COO critical path: can onboarding throughput, RM capacity, product fulfilment, and control assurance all scale at the same rate as accelerating inflows?
bullets: HSBC International Wealth and Premier Banking attracted US$39bn net new money in Q1 2026, with Asia Pacific generating US$34bn (87%); wealth fee income surged 15% YoY to $2.7bn
Total wealth balances reached $1.570 trillion (up 12% YoY); Asia holds $1.068 trillion — 68% of total — at a 14% growth pace that compounds execution pressure in H2

The H2 WPS risk is throughput: RM hiring, KYC and onboarding speed, product fulfilment velocity, and control assurance must all scale at the same rate as inflows to avoid regulatory concern or quality deterioration

talking_points.headline: OCBC Wealth Hits Record 39% of Group Profit — The Operational Model WPS Must Match
talking_points.why_it_matters: OCBC's Q1 2026 result — wealth contributing a record 39% of group income — represents what a fully operationalised wealth-centric operating model delivers at scale, and WPS change execution must close the gap while OCBC integrates HSBC's former Indonesia book.
bullets: OCBC wealth management income reached S$1.48bn in Q1 2026, up 11% YoY, with wealth contributing a record 39% of total group income; private banking fees alone rose 34% YoY to S$422 million

The structural driver is Singapore's family office incentives creating sustained UHNW AUM inflows — a systemic advantage that HSBC's APAC product and advisory model must counter with superior execution quality

OCBC is simultaneously integrating HSBC's former Indonesia wealth book (~S$6.6bn AUM, ~1,300 staff) — adding COO-level cross-border execution complexity that WPS should monitor as a competitive early-warning stress test on OCBC's own model

talking_points.headline: Agentic AI Governance Is 2026's Fastest-Moving Regulatory Risk — WPS Must Lead, Not Follow
talking_points.why_it_matters: With 44% of finance teams adopting agentic AI in 2026 and regulators across APAC issuing new cyber, AI, and ESG guidelines simultaneously, Surali's WPS governance framework must cover all four regulatory vectors before H2 adoption acceleration overwhelms control functions.
bullets: 44% of finance teams will use agentic AI in 2026 — a 600%+ year-over-year increase — but the industry lacks established AI governance standards, creating a regulatory window where WPS can define the HSBC standard rather than respond to one

APAC regulators issued concurrent governance requirements in Q1 2026: Japan finalised cyber risk preparedness guidelines, Taiwan updated remittance incident reporting rules, Indonesia mandated stronger IT and third-party bank oversight

The optimal WPS response is a single integrated AI governance framework — not four parallel workstreams — that connects AI agent deployment, cyber resilience, ESG reporting, and geopolitical fragmentation risk under one accountable COO-level programme
